hard.next_step: Follow up with Andre Mazzuccato on the AI-in-procurement materials shared after the discussion (iRisk, contract analysis/contracting agents); offer a scoping session with a solution consultant to tailor a demo to Symrise's priorities.
hard.ais_why: Rep is sending across the presentation plus case studies covering ANA, Contract Management, and Risk Management. Description is empty and there is no transcript or Avoma call. The ANA reference is rep-led, not customer-voice excitement. Qualified, $110K. Customer Andre is reviewing internally before scheduling a demo. Low engagement intensity.

north_star_verdict.headline: Qualified $110k S2C+Intake deal where the only buyer contact, Andre Mazzuccato, told Edward on 28 May 'we haven't decided for anything yet' and went quiet; no demo booked and no reply to Edward's 30 Jun chase. Momentum has thinned to a rep-side follow-up on a placeholder Apr 2027 close.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 9 Apr 2026, moved Initial Interest to Qualified 14 Apr 2026, now roughly 83 days in Qualified as of 6 Jul 2026. Close date 1 Apr 2027 is 269 days out (long runway, placeholder-shaped for an early deal). Last genuine buyer reply 28 May 2026 (Andre, non-committal); last touch is a rep-side email/call-attempt 30 Jun 2026. No slip on the record, but the deliverable that advances the deal (a scoping demo) has never been scheduled.
evidence: Stage moved Initial Interest to Qualified 14 Apr 2026 (field history), roughly 83 days in Qualified as of 6 Jul 2026 with no demo yet scheduled

Last real two-way buyer exchange was Andre Mazzuccato's 28 May 2026 email: 'As of now, we haven't decided for anything yet. As soon we have any news I will let you know' (SF Task, 28 Jun/28 May email received)
Edward Dlugosz sent chase emails 21 Apr, 27 May and 30 Jun 2026 with no substantive buyer reply after 28 May (SF activities)
No Avoma calls exist for this opp across opp, account and attendee-email discovery; the entire read rests on Salesforce activity and the Next Step field
Zero OpportunityContactRoles: no champion, decision maker or economic buyer is formally mapped on the record
Close date 1 Apr 2027 gives 269 days of runway, credible for an early-stage enterprise motion, so the issue is engagement, not the date
AIS 6.0 Moderate, and the AIS_Why notes the ANA interest is rep-led, not customer-voice excitement

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI target has been articulated by anyone at Symrise. The product scope (iContract, iRisk, Merlin Intake, ANA) points at contract lifecycle, contract risk and intake/requisition automation as the pain domains, and Edward Dlugosz's 10 Apr 2026 summary email frames the pitch around 'how AI can support procurement at Symrise,' but no buyer has stated a cost, cycle-time, headcount or risk-exposure number on any call or in any field (no Avoma calls exist and Customer_Business_Problem__c / Value_to_Customer__c are silent). The move is to run the scoping demo Edward already offered and get Andre Mazzuccato to size the current-state pain in his own numbers (contract volume, requisition backlog, risk incidents) so a savings case can be built.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified. There are zero OpportunityContactRoles, no budget-authority free-text field is populated, and no call exists to reveal who controls the $110k. The only engaged buyer on the record, Andre Mazzuccato, signs as 'Sr. Manager, Category Team Lead, United States' (SF Task email, 28 May 2026), a category-management role that is unlikely to own the commercial decision alone on a global S2C+Intake purchase. His own words, 'we haven't decided for anything yet,' point to an internal review above him that has not surfaced. The move is to ask Andre directly during the scoping session who signs off a procurement-technology purchase of this size and to earn an introduction to that owner.
sources: SF Task email 28 May 2026 (Andre Mazzuccato signature)

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer's evaluation criteria are undocumented. No RFP, scored requirement matrix or stated priority exists in any field, and no call captured what Symrise will judge vendors on. Edward's offer of case studies across ANA, Contract Management and Risk Management (Next Step, current) implies those three pillars are the interest areas, but that is the rep's framing, not a buyer-confirmed scorecard. Inferred criteria are contract automation depth, risk detection, intake/requisition AI (Merlin/ANA) and, for a global manufacturer, ERP integration, but none is confirmed. The move is to capture Andre's actual must-haves at the scoping demo and convert them into a written requirement list.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unknown beyond Andre Mazzuccato's statement that Symrise is reviewing internally and 'haven't decided for anything yet' (28 May 2026), which confirms a multi-person internal step but names no participants, gates or timeline. No field describes an approval chain and no call exists to read the process from. The 269-day runway to the Apr 2027 close suggests no fixed internal deadline is driving Symrise today. The move is to map the process at the scoping session: how they will evaluate, who participates, and what internal milestone (budget cycle, contract expiry) would force a decision.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are entirely unknown this early: no MSA/SOW discussion, no procurement-portal, legal or InfoSec detail, and no Current_Contract_Expiration__c forcing function on the record. Symrise is a large global ingredients manufacturer, so a formal procurement path with legal redlines and an InfoSec review is highly likely once a deal is live, but nothing is confirmed at Qualified stage. This is a normal gap for the stage, not a red flag. The move, once a demo lands and interest firms, is to ask Andre about Symrise's standard buying path and flag InfoSec and legal early.

identify_pain.narrative: No pain has been articulated by the buyer in their own words. The product scope (iContract, iRisk, Merlin Intake, ANA) implies contract-management inefficiency/risk and an intake/requisition bottleneck, and Edward's materials target 'AI to support procurement,' but Andre Mazzuccato has not confirmed any of it, saying only that they are still reviewing and have not decided. No Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c or Compelling_Event__c is populated, and no call exists. The inferred pain owner is Symrise's category/procurement operations team (Andre's function), but severity and urgency are unproven. The move is to make Andre describe a real current-state problem before pushing product.

champion.narrative: No champion exists. Andre Mazzuccato is the single engaged contact, but he is a passive point-of-contact, not an advocate: across three rep chases (21 Apr, 27 May, 30 Jun 2026) his one substantive reply was 'we haven't decided for anything yet' (28 May 2026), with no evidence of internal selling, introductions or agenda-driving. There are zero OpportunityContactRoles, so no one else is tied to the deal. Building a champion is the top priority, and it starts with converting Andre from a reviewer into an advocate at a scoping session, then widening to at least one more procurement stakeholder so the deal is not single-threaded through a non-committal manager.
sources: SF Task emails 21 Apr / 27 May / 28 May / 30 Jun 2026 (Andre Mazzuccato / Edward Dlugosz)

competition.narrative: No competitor is named in any source: Competitors__c is empty, no free-text competition field names a rival, and there are no Avoma calls to reveal a shortlist. On this deal the real threat is not a rival vendor but inertia. Andre's 'we haven't decided for anything yet, as soon we have any news I will let you know' (28 May 2026) is a classic status-quo posture: Symrise keeps handling contracts, risk and intake the way it does today and never opens a formal evaluation. As a global manufacturer Symrise almost certainly runs an incumbent contract/ERP stack (unconfirmed, likely SAP-based), which would become the competitor to displace once discovery happens. The move is to surface today's tools and any parallel look at the scoping session, and to create a reason to act now.

deal_movement.summary: Movement is rep-side, not buyer-side. After qualifying 14 Apr 2026, Edward Dlugosz worked a single email thread with Andre Mazzuccato; the buyer's only real reply (28 May) was non-committal and no demo was ever booked. The 30 Jun 2026 activity is another rep chase plus an internal reclassification (Division moved to New Markets), not buyer progress.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor has surfaced in any source (Competitors__c empty, no free-text rival, zero calls), so this is an early Qualified deal with no visible vendor fight. The single strongest current threat is do-nothing / status quo: Andre Mazzuccato said on 28 May 2026 'we haven't decided for anything yet,' signalling Symrise may simply keep managing contracts, risk and intake as-is rather than choose any vendor. A likely SAP-based incumbent stack (unconfirmed) would become the true competitor once discovery reveals it. Map the incumbent and any parallel evaluation at the first working session.

flags: Break the status-quo drift: Andre said 'we haven't decided for anything yet' on 28 May and has gone quiet, so land a tailored scoping demo that quantifies the cost of doing nothing.

Interest is rep-led, not customer-voice (AIS 6.0 Moderate); make Andre articulate a real pain and criteria before investing further presales.

critical_signals.text: The last real exchange was Andre Mazzuccato's 28 May reply, 'we haven't decided for anything yet,' after which he went quiet through a 30 Jun follow-up.
No rival is in play; the deal's real opponent is inertia, with Symrise showing no internal deadline to move against an Apr 2027 close.
Only one contact exists, category manager Andre Mazzuccato, and he is a passive reviewer we have not yet turned into an advocate.
The $110k has no confirmed budget owner and no buyer-stated ROI, so the price cannot yet be justified to anyone with authority.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: An early Qualified deal drifting on status quo: our only contact, category manager Andre Mazzuccato, said 'we haven't decided for anything yet' and went quiet, with no demo booked, no economic buyer mapped, and no rival in the fight, so the one thing between us and progress is turning topical AI interest into a real, sponsored evaluation.

win_position.text: This sits low because it is a single-threaded Qualified deal with no economic buyer, no champion and no buyer-stated pain; the only contact, Andre Mazzuccato, is a passive reviewer.
Andre told Edward on 28 May 2026 'we haven't decided for anything yet,' then did not reply to the 30 Jun chase, so the buyer has given no signal of preference for Zycus.
The AI appetite is rep-led not customer-voiced (AIS 6.0 Moderate, per AIS_Why), so there is no proven value case to anchor a win on.
The upside is that Symrise engaged on an AI-for-procurement pitch spanning iContract, iRisk, Merlin Intake and ANA, so there is topical interest to convert if a scoping demo lands.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is rep-side only: Edward chased on 21 Apr, 27 May and 30 Jun 2026 with just one non-committal buyer reply and no demo ever scheduled.
The engagement is still a not-yet-scheduled generic demo, with no senior stakeholders, dates or commercial topics entering the conversation.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer has committed to nothing: 'As of now, we haven't decided for anything yet. As soon we have any news I will let you know' (Andre Mazzuccato, 28 May 2026).
No requirement, timeline or next meeting has been requested by Symrise, and no one above Andre has been introduced.

deal_risk.text: Status-quo inertia is the primary risk: no compelling event and a distant Apr 2027 close mean Symrise can keep doing nothing indefinitely.
The deal is single-threaded through a category manager with no budget authority, so a single quiet contact can stall the whole opportunity.
